summary:
The combination of large anterior fontanelles, open sutures, slanting eyes, macroglossia, enamel hypoplasia, and delayed sexual development most closely aligns with Down syndrome. Delayed pubertal onset and enamel defects are well-documented features of this disorder, making it the best fit for all listed symptoms. 

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only two sentences.) Summary: The presence of large anterior fontanelles, open sutures, slanting eyes, macroglossia, enamel hypoplasia, and delayed sexual development strongly indicates Down syndrome. These specific findings—especially delayed puberty and dental abnormalities—are hallmark traits consistent across multiple sources linked to Down syndrome.
